title: Musk v. Altman closes as Vatican readies first AI encyclical - TCR 05/19/26
description:
A federal jury rejected Elon Musk's $150 billion suit against OpenAI and Sam Altman in under two hours on a statute-of-limitations finding, the Vatican announced Pope Leo's first encyclical on AI - Magnifica Humanitas - launching May 25 with Anthropic co-founder Christopher Olah on stage, and the NRC cleared X-energy's four Xe-100 reactors at Dow's Seadrift Texas complex through an environmental assessment for the first time in the agency's 52-year history.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-may-19-2026/

0:00 Musk v. Altman verdict in under two hours
1:31 A Two-Hour Verdict, Three Weeks of Testimony
4:12 Vatican readies first papal encyclical on AI
6:50 Pennsylvania model tariff for large-load customers
9:59 Anthropic acquires Stainless and consolidates SDK substrate
12:43 Close

This episode covers the Musk v. Altman verdict and what three weeks of sworn testimony from Mira Murati and Greg Brockman put into the federal record, the Vatican's first papal encyclical on AI with a frontier interpretability researcher on the launch stage, SandboxAQ moving physics-grounded quantum chemistry models inside Claude, the NRC's proportional review finding for X-energy's gas-cooled reactors, Pennsylvania's first-of-its-kind model tariff sending grid costs back to large-load data center customers, and Anthropic's $300 million Stainless acquisition consolidating the shared SDK substrate that OpenAI, Google, and Cloudflare had been building on.

The Musk v. Altman jury closed three weeks of testimony in under two hours on a procedural deadline, leaving Mira Murati's account of Altman misrepresenting legal clearance, Greg Brockman's 2017 Hillsborough meeting testimony, and the "prolific liar" cross-examination in the permanent federal record. The next founding charter at an AI lab gets drafted by people who know that transcript exists. The Vatican will release Magnifica Humanitas on May 25, signed 135 years to the day after Leo XIII's Rerum Novarum, with Christopher Olah representing mechanistic interpretability work on the launch stage alongside theologians Anna Rowlands and Léocadie Lushombo. The encyclical is expected to call for prohibition of lethal autonomous weapons and treat labor displacement as a question of human dignity. SandboxAQ's large quantitative models - grounded in Schrödinger's equation and molecular dynamics rather than internet-text consensus - now reach research chemists through Claude conversation rather than specialist pipelines. The NRC cleared X-energy's four-reactor Seadrift project through an environmental assessment, the first such finding in 52 years, reviving a high-temperature gas-cooled reactor class the U.S. abandoned in 1989. Pennsylvania's model tariff recommends large-load customers above 50 MW pay up front for any grid infrastructure that would not exist "but for" their connection. Anthropic acquired Stainless for over $300 million and will discontinue the hosted SDK generator several frontier labs had been building on, internalizing a shared substrate into one proprietary stack.
